facility_name: Dawn AI Supercomputer -- University of Cambridge
operator_company: University of Cambridge Research Computing Services
full_address: West Cambridge Data Centre, University of Cambridge, Cambridge, UK
build_phase_if_known: Phase 1 operational Feb 2024; Phase 2 expansion funded (36M GBP)
cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling (DLC) via Dell PowerEdge XE9640 -- 60kW DLC per rack, up to 80kW total per rack
opportunity_summary: UK's fastest AI supercomputer. 256 Dell PowerEdge XE9640 nodes with 512 Intel Xeon Scalable CPUs + 1,024 Intel Data Center GPU Max 1550 accelerators. Direct liquid cooling handles 60kW per rack. PUE 1.14. Phase 2 expansion with AMD MI355X GPUs funded at 36M GBP. Part of Cambridge Open Zettascale Lab.
confidence_reason: DCD, Intel Newsroom, and Cambridge HPC page all confirm DLC, PUE 1.14, Dell XE9640 nodes. Phase 2 expansion confirmed by IT Pro and University of Cambridge.
source_1_title: Breaking Dawn: Inside the UK's newest AI supercomputer
source_1_evidence_snippet: Each advanced rack handles up to 80kW, including 60kW via direct liquid cooling (DLC). A fully redundant Secondary Fluid Network (SFN) was deployed. PUE score of 1.14.
source_2_title: UK's Fastest AI Supercomputer, Powered by Intel and Dell Technologies
source_2_evidence_snippet: 512 4th Generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors and 1,024 Intel Data Center GPU Max 1550 accelerators on 256 Dell PowerEdge XE9640 server nodes.
notes: Part of AIRR program. Phase 2 will add AMD MI355X GPUs for sixfold power capacity increase. Cambridge Open Zettascale Lab hosts the system.
expansion_details: Phase 2: AMD MI355X GPUs, sixfold power capacity increase, 36M GBP funding
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Dawn will receive additional 36M GBP funding for sixfold power capacity increase with AMD MI355X accelerators.

facility_name: Alps Supercomputer -- CSCS / ETH Zurich
full_address: CSCS, Via Trevano 131, 6900 Lugano, Switzerland
cooling_type: 100% fanless direct liquid cooling (HPE Cray EX) + Lake Lugano water cooling at 45m depth, pumped 2.8km to facility
opportunity_summary: Europe's 2nd most powerful supercomputer. 10,752 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. 270 petaflops. 100% fanless DLC via HPE Cray EX254n cabinets. Lake Lugano water cooling from 45m depth. 10MW at full load (25MW peak capacity). PUE < 1.25. Waste heat used for Lugano district heating. Micro-turbines on return water generate 200 MWh/year.
confidence_reason: ETH Zurich press release, CSCS official page, Wikipedia all confirm GH200 chips, DLC, lake water cooling, 10MW power. CSCS fact sheet confirms PUE < 1.25 and micro-turbine system.
source_1_title: New research infrastructure: Alps supercomputer inaugurated
source_1_evidence_snippet: 10,752 NVIDIA Grace Hopper superchips. 270 petaflops. Power consumption at full load is 10 MW.
source_2_title: At CSCS, energy efficiency is a key priority, even at high performance
source_2_evidence_snippet: Water for cooling taken from Lake Lugano at 45m depth, pumped 2.8 km. Data centre designed to cool up to 14 megawatts. PUE < 1.25.
notes: Lake Lugano water at 45m depth provides natural cooling. Return water drives micro-turbines producing 200 MWh/year (covers 30% of pumping station energy). Waste heat sent to Lugano district heating. CSCS is extending Alps infrastructure across Switzerland.

expansion_details: CSCS extending Alps infrastructure across Switzerland

opportunity_summary: Europe's first exascale supercomputer. 1 ExaFLOP/s (FP64). 24,000 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. 125 Eviden BullSequana XH3000 liquid-cooled racks. 36,000+ cables. 2,300 sqm footprint. #1 Green500 June 2025 for JEDI module. Waste heat planned for campus heating network. 90 exaflops AI capacity.

facility_name: MPCDF Supercomputer (Cobra successor) -- Max Planck Society
operator_company: Max Planck Computing and Data Facility (MPCDF)
full_address: MPCDF, Giessenbach-strasse 2, 85748 Garching bei Muenchen, Germany
build_phase_if_known: CPU nodes delivered Q3 2023; full deployment first half 2024
cooling_type: 100% fanless hot water Direct Liquid Cooling (Atos/Eviden BullSequana XH3000) -- no fans, warm water DLC
opportunity_summary: Max Planck Society's primary supercomputer. 10 BullSequana XH3000 racks, 768 CPU nodes + 192 GPU nodes. AMD EPYC 4th Gen + AMD Instinct MI300A GPUs (European Atos-first). 100% fanless DLC with hot water. PUE < 1.05. 20M EUR contract. 3x performance over predecessor Cobra. Serves all 86 Max Planck Institutes.
confidence_reason: Atos/Eviden press release, HPCwire, DCD, The Register all confirm BullSequana XH3000, fanless DLC, AMD MI300A GPUs, and PUE < 1.05.
source_1_title: Atos to build Max Planck Society's new BullSequana XH3000-based supercomputer
source_1_evidence_snippet: System will run without fans thanks to unmatched hot water cooling (Direct Liquid Cooling). PUE value to less than 1.05. 768 processor nodes and 192 accelerator nodes.
source_2_title: Atos to build $21.5m supercomputer for German science and technology organization
source_2_evidence_snippet: Total order value of over 20 million euros. Hosted at MPCDF in Garching. Fanless hot water DLC.
notes: Predecessor systems: Raven (2020, Lenovo), Cobra (2018). First European Atos-based system with AMD Instinct MI300A. Two-stage liquid cooling: direct CPU/GPU cooling + Lenovo rear-door heat exchangers on earlier Raven system.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Fully deployed in the first half of 2024, with CPU nodes delivered first in Q3 of 2023.

full_address: RIKEN Center for Computational Science, 7-1-26 Minatojima-minami-machi, Chuo-ku, Kobe, 650-0047, Japan

cooling_type: Direct water cooling (90% water, 10% air ratio). Dual-circuit system: secondary water directly cools ~160,000 CPUs using pure water + corrosion inhibitor. Fujikura liquid cooling units (LCU) with center-flow-reversal design.

Fujikura LCUs with innovative center-flow design for improved cooling velocity. 30-40MW power. Heat density >100kW/sqm. FugakuNEXT successor planned for 2030 with liquid-cooled NVIDIA Blackwell + Fujitsu MONAKA-X CPUs at 40MW.

opportunity_summary: Fastest supercomputer in Southern Hemisphere. 50 PFLOP/s peak. 217,088 AMD compute cores + 768 GPU cores. 1,792 nodes across 8 HPE Cray EX cabinets. DLC with CSIRO-pioneered geothermal cooling saves 7M litres water/year. #4 Green500 (56.983 GFlops/watt). 120kW solar panels. 30x more powerful than predecessors with only 50% power increase. $48M AUD system.

opportunity_summary: Swedish NAISS flagship supercomputer. HPE Cray EX with CDU liquid cooling. 1,278 CPU nodes (AMD EPYC Zen2) + 62 GPU nodes. 13.5 PFLOP/s. Heat recovery into KTH campus heating (100% renewable cooling). HPE Slingshot interconnect. Multiple RAM tiers (256GB to 2TB per node).

full_address: Sandia National Laboratories, Albuquerque, NM 87185

cooling_type: Single-phase immersion cooling (Submer system). Biodegradable, nontoxic, nonflammable, noncorrosive dielectric fluid. Entire server racks submerged in immersion tanks.
opportunity_summary: DOE national laboratory testing Submer single-phase immersion cooling for HPC. Expected 70% energy reduction vs conventional cooling. 100% heat capture eliminates fans and chillers. Biodegradable, nontoxic dielectric fluid. Partnered through Adacen Inc. (Albuquerque). Evaluating hardware reliability and infrastructure cost. Formal case study planned.

facility_name: MIT Lincoln Laboratory Supercomputing Center (LLSC)
full_address: MGHPCC, 100 Bigelow Street, Holyoke, MA 01040
cooling_type: Housed at MGHPCC (LEED Platinum) -- high-efficiency cooling plant supporting up to 10MW compute. Water in-row chillers. 70% hydroelectric power from Connecticut River. Birthplace of JetCool microconvective liquid cooling technology (Project JETS).
opportunity_summary: World's largest interactive on-demand supercomputing center. Houses TX-GAIA (4.7 PFLOP/s). Hosted at MGHPCC (LEED Platinum, first academic DC with that status). 15MW utility connection, 70% hydroelectric. 10MW compute capacity. Birthplace of JetCool microconvective DLC technology (Project JETS, spun out 2019, acquired by Flex 2024). Also houses Harvard Cannon cluster.
confidence_reason: MIT Lincoln Lab official pages, MGHPCC site, NVIDIA blog, and JetCool About page confirm LLSC, TX-GAIA, MGHPCC specs, and JetCool origin story.
